summary: The IRGC launched a drone attack on the U.S. Fifth Fleet in Bahrain, while the United States conducted a second wave of strikes in Iran. Israel bombed Hamas's naval police headquarters in Khan Younis, and Hezbollah fired a rocket at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon that was intercepted. Iranian officials warned of further retaliation against U.S. actions in the region.
key_points: IRGC naval forces carried out a drone attack on the U.S. Fifth Fleet stationed in Bahrain at 02:30 AM local time (2300Z). (Source: @shin_persian/6828)
A senior U.S. official reported a second wave of U.S. attacks in Iran is occurring now. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3715)
The Israeli Air Force struck the main headquarters of Hamas's naval police in the Khan Younis area, killing several operatives including commander Ismail al‑Lahham. (Source: @manniefabian/62013)
Hezbollah launched a rocket toward Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; it was intercepted by Israeli air defenses. (Source: @manniefabian/58531)
An explosion was reported in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province, described by an eyewitness as a strike. (Source: @shin_persian/6825)
U.S. House Speaker Mike Johnson said he was notified of defensive strikes against Iran while in the Situation Room with President Trump and others. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3710)
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that Iran will leave no attack or threat unanswered and urged foreign forces to leave the region. (Source: @presstv/193394)

sections.executive_summary: The IRGC's drone strike on the U.S. Fifth Fleet in Bahrain marks a direct escalation, followed by a second wave of U.S. attacks inside Iran. Israel intensified its campaign by bombing Hamas's naval police headquarters in Khan Younis, while Hezbollah engaged Israeli forces in southern Lebanon. Iranian leadership issued stark warnings of broader retaliation against U.S. and allied actions.

points: IRGC naval forces launched a drone attack on the U.S. Fifth Fleet in Bahrain at 02:30 AM Tehran time, stating that further severe responses will follow if provocations continue. (Source: @shin_persian/6828)
A senior U.S. official confirmed a second wave of U.S. attacks inside Iran is underway, indicating ongoing U.S. offensive operations. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3715)
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared that Iran's armed forces will leave no attack or threat unanswered and urged foreign powers to leave the region for safety. (Source: @presstv/193394)
Iranian officials reported hearing explosions again in Jask County, suggesting continued hostilities near the Persian Gulf. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3713)
Iranian sources claimed that U.S. forces attacked several Iranian air‑defense and radar systems around the Hormoz Island area. (Source: @New_security8200/201534)

The Israeli Air Force bombed the main headquarters of Hamas's naval police in the Khan Younis area, killing several terror operatives including commander Ismail al‑Lahham. (Source: @manniefabian/62013)

Israel reported that a Hezbollah‑launched rocket toward its troops in southern Lebanon was intercepted by Israeli air defenses, triggering an early warning in the Kiryat Shmona area. (Source: @manniefabian/58531)

A gunman infiltrated from Lebanon into the Ramim Ridge area of Israeli territory, opened fire on troops, and was subsequently shot dead with no Israeli injuries reported. (Source: @manniefabian/58527)
Israeli officials noted that the situation around the Hormoz Island area had temporarily calmed according to Iranian media reports. (Source: @N12chat/211980)
Israel’s IDF announced the destruction of three Hamas arms caches and the killing of several operatives attempting to move weapons from warehouses in Gaza. (Source: @manniefabian/62013)
The Israeli Air Force strike on Hamas's naval police headquarters in Khan Younis resulted in multiple terrorist casualties, including a senior cell commander. (Source: @manniefabian/62013)
Israel reported destroying three Hamas weapons caches in Gaza earlier in the week, after warning residents and identifying operatives moving the weapons. (Source: @manniefabian/62013)
No civilian injuries were reported in the Khan Younis strike, according to the IDF statement. (Source: @manniefabian/62013)
Hezbollah fired a rocket at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; Israeli air defenses intercepted the projectile, and an early warning was issued for Kiryat Shmona. (Source: @manniefabian/58531)
A gunman who entered Israeli territory from Lebanon and opened fire on troops in the Ramim Ridge area was killed by Israeli forces, with no injuries to soldiers. (Source: @manniefabian/58527)
Uncensored reports indicated explosions in southern Lebanon and on the island of Keshm, though details were sparse. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92802)
Lebanese authorities established a security cordon after a rocket fragment landed on the Arab Highway between Tannayel and Bar Elias. (Source: @redlinkleb/23809)
PressTV reported drones spotted over Iraq, indicating possible aerial activity linked to regional tensions. (Source: @presstv/193400)
A senior U.S. official told CNN that recent U.S. strikes are intended as warnings and are not expected to derail negotiations to end the war. (Source: @VahidOnline/76114)
Iranian statements warned of a strong response to any further U.S. aggression in the Persian Gulf region. (Source: @naya_foriraq/78027)
IRGC forces claimed a drone attack on the U.S. Fifth Fleet in Bahrain, signaling direct confrontation with Gulf security assets. (Source: @shin_persian/6828)
Iranian officials threatened broader attacks on U.S. bases in the region if further aggression occurs. (Source: @naya_foriraq/78027)
Explosions were reported in Bahrain, though details were limited. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92806)
Qatar reportedly closed its airspace, according to a rapid alert post. (Source: @kianmeli1/87824)

key_developments: The IRGC launched a drone attack on the U.S. Fifth Fleet stationed in Bahrain at 02:30 AM local time, warning of more severe responses if provocations continue. (Source: @shin_persian/6828)
A senior U.S. official confirmed a second wave of U.S. attacks inside Iran is currently underway, indicating escalating U.S. offensive actions. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3715)
Israel bombed Hamas's naval police headquarters in Khan Younis, killing several operatives including commander Ismail al‑Lahham. (Source: @manniefabian/62013)
Hezbollah fired a rocket toward Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; Israeli air defenses intercepted it, triggering an early warning in Kiryat Shmona. (Source: @manniefabian/58531)
An explosion was reported in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province, described by an eyewitness as a strike. (Source: @shin_persian/6825)
U.S. House Speaker Mike Johnson said he was notified of defensive strikes against Iran while in the Situation Room with President Trump and other officials. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3710)
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that Iran will leave no attack or threat unanswered and urged foreign forces to leave the region for safety. (Source: @presstv/193394)
sections.threat_assessment: Iran is escalating its direct confrontation with U.S. forces, demonstrated by the IRGC drone strike on the Bahrain Fifth Fleet and vocal threats of broader retaliation. The United States is responding with successive strike waves inside Iran, raising the risk of further escalation. Israel's intensified operations against Hamas in Gaza and defensive actions against Hezbollah in Lebanon add multiple fronts of conflict, increasing regional instability.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states are reacting to Iranian aggression with heightened alertness, as seen in Qatar's airspace closure and reports of explosions in Bahrain. Lebanese authorities have imposed security cordons following rocket incidents, while Israeli forces continue to intercept Hezbollah attacks. International actors, including the U.S. congressional leadership, are monitoring the situation closely, indicating potential diplomatic pressure alongside military actions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of IRGC drone capabilities and launch sites is critical, given the recent attack on the U.S. Fifth Fleet. Continued U.S. strike activity in Iran suggests a sustained campaign that may target additional strategic assets. Israeli and Hezbollah engagements in southern Lebanon require close observation for escalation patterns, and the reported explosions in Ahvaz may indicate further covert operations within Iran.
